question: A C++ HoloHub application now fails in ./holohub build after I changed its operator dependency. Find the first causal error and make the smallest fix while preserving my unrelated edits.
ground_truth: The agent freezes the existing checkout and exact command, previews and reproduces the failure, isolates metadata/CMake/dependency/image/compiler boundaries, tests one hypothesis at a time, changes only the owning root cause, and validates with the identical build plus focused tests and lint.
expected_behavior: Records the full revision and dirty baseline without cloning, switching revisions, or disturbing unrelated changes
Uses --dryrun --verbose on the exact build shape before real reproduction
Uses the first actionable CMake or compiler failure rather than final shutdown noise
Checks metadata, registration, operator targets, include/link paths, language, mode, and build type as separate boundaries
Does not reset, clear caches, upgrade dependencies, use host-local execution, or commit without authorization

question: ./holohub install my-module --dev succeeded, but the Python used by my consumer still cannot import it. Diagnose the environment mismatch.
ground_truth: The agent treats this as an environment-scoped editable-hook failure. It records ./holohub environment identity and the hook target, compares it with the consumer environment/container, changes only the mismatched selection or consumer path, verifies the import, and removes stale diagnostic hooks.
expected_behavior: Activates holohub-debug-build-run rather than the non-failing module lifecycle
Uses ./holohub env-info and inspects the exact editable-hook destination with sensitive paths redacted
Compares the selected and consuming environments instead of changing module source first
Does not assume a host editable hook is forwarded into a project container
Verifies import in the corrected environment and removes stale diagnostic hooks
